title: Palhinhaea trianae
intro: Palhinhaea trianae, also known as the Triana's club moss or Palhinhaea, is a small, evergreen fern-like plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Palhinhaea trianae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as trianicine and palhinhaein, which have been shown to exhibit potent biological activities. In herbal medicine, Palhinhaea trianae is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to alleviate symptoms of colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions.
taxonomy: Palhinhaea trianae is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a member of the genus Palhinhaea, which is a group of small to medium-sized, delicate ferns. The Hymenophyllaceae family is a group of filmy ferns, which are known for their thin, delicate fronds. These ferns are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. They are epiphytic or terrestrial plants that thrive in shaded, humid environments.
